commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=b590534968a7b29eea6d005fd3dadf7745d4a76e)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds canonical session `title` support across gateway schemas, create/list/patch/resolve, durable `SessionEntry` metadata, `/name` handling, UI session display types, plugin slot reservation, and tests while mirroring legacy `label`.

PR surface: Source +92, Tests +71. Total +163 across 18 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for source-level review: current main is label-only, while PR head adds the title/clear paths and regression tests. There is still no posted live after-fix runtime proof.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review remains needed because product/compatibility acceptance and contributor real behavior proof cannot be supplied by a repair lane.

target.title: fix(sessions): migrate session naming to canonical title
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Session naming was partly migrated from legacy `label` to canonical user-editable `title`, but the runtime contract was incomplete. New gateway schemas accepted `title` while `sessions.create` dropped it, `sessions.list` did not project or filter it, and the durable `SessionEntry` type did not declare the persisted field. That left new title-based clients and older label-based clients able to disagree about the same session name.

Additionally, two regressions existed in the patch path:

1. `sessions.patch({ label: null })` and `sessions.patch({ title: null })` crashed because `normalizeSessionTitleText(null)` calls `null.trim()` before the null-clear branch.
2. Mixed null/string pairs like `{ title: null, label: "foo" }` were silently accepted — the null won and cleared both fields, ignoring the value the client intended to set.

## Why This Change Was Made

This completes the title contract while preserving the legacy label mirror:

- `sessions.create` now passes `title` into the shared patch applier, which mirrors it to `label` for compatibility.
- `SessionEntry` now declares `title?: string` as durable session metadata.
- Gateway session rows now project normalized `title` from `title` or legacy `label`.
- `sessions.list({ title })` now filters by the same normalized title comparison used by patch and resolve.
- Session list search includes the canonical title field.
- Nullable `title: null` and `label: null` clears are preserved as sentinels before string normalization.
- Mixed null/string title+label patch pairs are explicitly rejected as ambiguous.
- Focused tests cover `/name`, create, patch, resolve, list projection/filtering, legacy label compatibility, nullable clears, and mixed-pair rejections.

## User Impact

Users and clients can set, clear, list, create, patch, and resolve session names through the canonical `title` field without breaking existing `label` consumers. Legacy label-only session entries still appear as titled rows and remain resolvable through title-based lookup. Session name clears via `sessions.patch({ label: null })` and `sessions.patch({ title: null })` now work correctly. Mixed clear/set patch pairs are rejected with a clear error instead of silently dropping the set value.

## Evidence

Head SHA: `b590534968`

### Regression fix 1: nullable clear sentinels normalized before null check

**Bug:** `sessions-patch.ts:362-363` called `normalizeSessionTitleText()` on patch values whenever they were not `undefined`, including `null`. This caused `null.trim()` to throw before the clear branch.

**Fix:** Check for `null` explicitly. `null` passes through as a sentinel; string values are still normalized with `trim().normalize("NFC")`.

### Regression fix 2: mixed null/string title+label pairs silently ignoring set values

**Bug:** `{ title: null, label: "foo" }` or `{ title: "foo", label: null }` — the null side won via `const raw = rawTitle !== undefined ? rawTitle : rawLabel`, and `applySessionTitle(next, null)` cleared both fields, silently dropping the string the client intended to set.

**Fix:** Explicitly reject when one field is `null` and the other is a non-null string:

```typescript
if (
  (rawTitle === null && rawLabel !== undefined && rawLabel !== null) ||
  (rawLabel === null && rawTitle !== undefined && rawTitle !== null)
) {
  return invalid("cannot clear title or label while setting the other");
}
```

### Regression tests added

```
✓ clears title and label when patch sets title: null
✓ clears title and label when patch sets label: null
✓ clears title and label when both title: null and label: null
✓ rejects title: null with label set to a string
✓ rejects label: null with title set to a string
✓ rejects title: null with label set to a different string when both provided
✓ rejects title set to a string with label: null when both provided
```

### All touched tests pass on current head (b590534968)

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/sessions-patch.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-name.test.ts src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.create.test.ts src/gateway/sessions-resolve.test.ts
```

```
Gateway shard:  Test Files 14 passed (14), Tests 734 passed (734)
Auto-reply shard:  Test Files 1 passed (1), Tests 7 passed (7)
Total: 741 passed in 133.68s
```

### Lint gate

`check-lint` passes on current head. Previous head (`6d05bb66ac`) failed with `TS2344` on `session-entry-slot-keys.ts` — fixed by adding `"title"` to `SESSION_ENTRY_RESERVED_SLOT_KEY_LIST`.

### ClawSweeper P2 findings addressed (from previous review)

1. **sessions-patch.ts: compare title/label after parsing** — Normalized with `trim().normalize("NFC")` before comparison.
2. **session-display.ts: add title to shared UI row contract** — `GatewaySessionRow` in `ui/src/ui/types.ts` now declares `title?: string`.

### Mantis live proof requested

`@openclaw-mantis` has been asked to capture Telegram live proof for `/name <title>`, `/name --clear`, and gateway title/label round-trip agreement (see PR comments).

@clawsweeper re-review
target.author: jason-allen-oneal

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
